headline: Inflation Adjusted Dividend Income: How to Replace $80,000 in Today’s Dollars 20 Years From Now
summary: A 50-year-old woman planning to retire at 70 on the equivalent of $80,000 in today’s purchasing power is actually targeting a much larger nominal income figure. Assuming long-run inflation averages 3% annually, maintaining that same lifestyle 20 years from now would require roughly $144,500 per year in nominal dollars.
